analysis.headline: Total net P&L over 14 ET days is +$861.53 on 2,858 trades (fees $1,273.46; gross +$2,134.99 → fees eat 59.7% of gross). The decomposition is stark: resolution exits made +$3,772.06 (n=1383, positive all 14/14 days, bootstrap p10 +$2,846) while scalp exits lost -$2,910.53 (n=1475, positive 0/14 days, p90 -$2,129). Directionally-wrong trades (chosen side lost the window) cost -$4,097.83 — 4.8x the total profit — and the entry-edge slope test confirms claimed edge does not realize: per-day OLS slope of (won - entry_price) on edge is -0.036 (t_day=-0.15) calibrated and -0.084 (t_day=-0.34) raw over 14 days, i.e. zero (even mildly inverted: win rate FALLS from 0.711 to 0.567 as claimed edge rises, because mean entry price falls faster). The bot's profit comes entirely from trades it held to resolution when right (+$5,427); the scalp engine is net-negative in both agreement states (-$463 when right, -$2,443 when wrong).

fee_sanity_check.verdict: pnl IS net of fees; one-sided residual = recorded entry_price vs actual (worse) fill price

caveats: Only TWO exit_reason values exist in the data: 'scalp' and 'resolution'. No loss-cut variant appears as a distinct exit_reason, and zero counterfactual records carry a loss_cut flag in context_at_scalp — the loss-cut branch either never fired in this 14-day window or folds into 'scalp' unflagged. The 'by exit type' table therefore has no loss-cut row.

Duplicates: 0 in outcomes (2858 raw = 2858 unique; the 2854 double-write was in counterfactuals, not outcomes). 298 dropped in counterfactuals — but these are NOT byte-identical double-writes: each is a pair of one scalp-arm CF plus one hold-arm CF whose actual.pnl matches a NEIGHBORING position's outcome (mis-keyed position_id in the hold-arm writer; e.g. CF pid 2806 hold-arm carries outcome 2807's pnl). All 298 belong to scalped outcomes. Naive keep-latest dedupe would lose the window winner for 7 positions (hold-arm written later), so the winner map is built from ALL raw scalp-arm CF records instead (0 resolution_price conflicts across any pid); dedupe-by-latest is still applied for record counting. This mis-keyed hold-arm CF writer is a possible production bug worth flagging upstream.

24 scalped trades (of 1,475) lack any scalp-arm CF record, so their window winner is unknowable; they are excluded from agreement/win-rate/slope analyses only (their -$5.02 pnl stays in exit-type and fee totals). 22 have no CF record at all, 2 (pids 436, 2731) have only the mis-keyed hold-arm.

Fee sanity check residual is 1.4% of size mean (max 4.1%), one-sided (+0.50% signed): reconstructing pnl from the recorded entry_price overstates it because the actual fill price (paper-mode slippage/tick-snap) was slightly worse. pnl on disk is internally consistent as net-of-fees at the real fill; per the stated convention pnl was used as-is, no re-costing.

Day-clustered bootstrap resamples the GLOBAL 14-day list with replacement (seed 42, 1000 iters) for every group, so a group absent on a sampled day contributes $0 that iteration — CIs are comparable across groups. n=14 days is a thin clustering base; CIs are directional, not precise.

Edge buckets <0, 0-0.02, 0.02-0.04 are empty (1 raw-edge record in 0.02-0.04) by construction — the min_edge=0.04 entry gate censors the pool below 0.04, so the slope test only spans edge 0.04..~0.4 and cannot speak to sub-gate edges.

The 'win%' column in the exit-type table is pnl>0 per trade (a scalp can profit while the side loses); directional correctness lives in the agreement section. agree+disagree+unknown pnl sums to the total (4964.37-4097.83-5.02=861.52, rounding).
A live paper bot was appending during analysis; data was loaded once at script start (2,858 outcomes, through position 2860 / 2026-06-10 ~21:18 UTC).

discrepancies: CAVEAT (dup-CF forensics): claims naive keep-latest dedupe would lose the window winner for 7 positions (hold-arm written later). Actual: 5 (pids 314, 762, 1122, 1936, 2710), by both their own dedupe rule and strict timestamp comparison. 7 is reachable only by conflating the 2 only-hold-arm pids (436, 2731), which dedupe cannot 'lose' (no scalp-arm ever existed). Zero numeric impact — the winner map is built from all raw scalp-arm CFs, which handles all cases.

CAVEAT (mis-keyed hold-arm hypothesis): claims 'each [of the 298 dup pairs is] ... one hold-arm CF whose actual.pnl matches a NEIGHBORING position's outcome'. Actual: 249/298 hold-arm pnls match SOME other pid's outcome pnl (<0.005), but only 102 within +-1 pid and 134 within +-10; 49/298 match no outcome at all; many 'matches' are 1000+ pids away (coincidental value collisions). The cited example (CF 2806 hold-arm pnl 6.7704 == outcome 2807 pnl) verifies exactly, and all 298 are (hold,scalp) pairs under scalped outcomes as claimed — but 'each matches a neighboring outcome' is an overreach; the mis-key bug hypothesis is demonstrated for ~1/3 of pairs, plausible but unproven for the rest.

CAVEAT (loss-cut): claims 'zero counterfactual records carry a loss_cut flag in context_at_scalp'. Actual: 14 CF records carry context_at_scalp.loss_cut = False; zero carry True. The substantive conclusion (loss-cut branch never fired in the window; no loss-cut exit row) stands, but the flag key does exist on disk.

DEFINITION (disclosed in script, not in result JSON): 'edge' in the buckets and slope test is model_probability - entry_price (the FILL price), not the task convention's chosen-side MARKET price (market_price_up/down). All claimed numbers reproduce exactly under their definition (slope cal -0.0356/t -0.15, raw -0.0836/t -0.34; buckets 556/1448/854). Under the convention definition the result is the same-or-stronger: slope cal -0.098 (t_day -0.38), raw -0.147 (t_day -0.52), n_days=14; buckets n=607/1465/786 with win rate 0.712 -> 0.562. Conclusion (claimed edge does not realize; mild inversion) is robust to the definition.

HEADLINE PHRASING (numbers correct, causal wording garbled): 'win rate FALLS from 0.711 to 0.567 ... because mean entry price falls faster' — across their buckets win rate fell by 0.144 while mean entry fell by 0.114, i.e. win rate fell slightly FASTER than entry price (realized edge wr-entry: +0.028 -> -0.002). The cited 0.711/0.567 and the zero-realization conclusion are correct.
verification.notes:
Independent re-derivation in scripts/goal_eval/verify_p1_pnl_decomposition.py (written and run BEFORE reading their script) plus convention-replication pass scripts/goal_eval/verify_p1_pass2.py. Pool identical to theirs (no mid-flight appends to outcomes: 2858 raw, max pid 2860, last ts 2026-06-10T21:18:41Z; CFs 2801 raw -> 2503 unique).

EVERY key_number verified EXACTLY: totals (+861.53 / fees 1273.46 / gross 2134.99 / ratio 0.5965 / n 2858 / 14 days), dedupe (0 outcomes, 298 CFs), exit-type table incl. fees, win%, days_pos, and bootstrap p10/p90; agreement splits (1743/+4964.37, 1091/-4097.83, 24/-5.02) and all 5 cross-tab cells; 24/22/2 CF-coverage counts; 0 resolution_price conflicts; slope tests; edge buckets; total_boot (851.25/229.44/1460.30); fee_sanity (1.413/4.06/+0.503). Internal consistency holds: 3772.06-2910.53=861.53; 4964.37-4097.83-5.02=861.52; 673+1070=1743; 778+313=1091; bucket nets sum to 861.54.

METHODOLOGY AUDIT — all checks pass: (1) no sign errors; per-day table confirms resolution positive 14/14 (min +2.10 on 05-30), scalp negative 14/14 (max -32.38). (2) No double-counting: outcomes per-trade and rollup files do not overlap (0 dups in 2858 raw); CF dedupe correct and, critically, the window-winner map is built from ALL raw scalp-arm CFs (1451 pids = 1475 scalps - 24 unknown, 0 conflicts), avoiding the keep-latest trap. (3) Day clustering is real: bootstrap resamples the global 14-day list (seed 42, re-seeded per group -> comparable CIs, confirmed by exact reproduction); slope t = mean(per-day OLS slopes)/(pstdev/sqrt(14)), genuinely clustered, n_days=14 reported. (4) Fees: pnl used as-is (net); gross = net + fees only; fee formula spot-check 0.07*shares*p*(1-p) at recorded entry matches disk fees to <0.0001 on sampled trades; fee-sanity residual direction (+0.5% signed, recon-from-recorded-entry overstates pnl) confirmed on all 1383 resolution trades (+0.527%). (5) No chosen-side/P(up) domain confusion: model_probability(_raw) treated as chosen-side throughout; market price only enters via the (disclosed) entry_price substitution — see discrepancies. (6) Scalp window winner recovered via CF resolution_price, never outcome.correct. (7) Censoring caveat accurate: cal-edge buckets below 0.04 empty; raw-edge has exactly 1 record in 0.02-0.04 as claimed; only exit_reason values are scalp/resolution.

Bootstrap RNG nuance: claimed percentiles require their exact procedure (random.seed(42) before EACH group, random.choices(k=14), p10=sorted[100], p90=sorted[899]); a different-but-valid stream (precomputed samples, random.choice loop, p90=sorted[900]) shifts results by ~$5-70, within MC noise — not an error, but the numbers are procedure-pinned.

analysis.headline: No segment survives. Across 44 pool-A segment tests (22 buckets x 2 predictors) on 2,829 trades over 14 ET days, zero segments meet the pass bar (n>=30, day-clustered t>2 model-better, >=3 positive days). The market beats the model OVERALL in every pool/predictor combination: pool A raw t=-3.66, pool A L1-only t=-3.10, pool B raw t=-4.35, pool B L1 t=-3.77 (sign convention: positive = model better). The only positive-mean pockets are statistical noise: 'trending_up' (n=29, below the n>=30 bar, t=0.81), 'volatile' (n=242, t=0.46-0.62), ATR top tercile (t=0.11-0.17), and trend-regime L1 (mean +0.0005, t=0.08) — none approach t>2, and with 44 tests 1-2 marginal survivors would be expected by chance anyway. The Phase 1 conclusion (model loses to market price) holds in every pocket large enough to measure; it is in fact STRONGER here (t≈3.7 market-better) than the Phase 1 estimate (t=2.23).

segment tests run on pool A (bucket x predictor): 44

caveats: Sign convention everywhere: mean_diff = brier_market - brier_model per the spec, so POSITIVE = model better; all overall values are negative (market better). The overall market-better t here (3.66 raw, pool A) is larger than Phase 1's 2.23 — different pool construction (position_id dedupe, scalp outcomes recovered via CF resolution_price, 29 scalps without CF excluded), same direction.
29 scalped trades excluded from pool A because no counterfactual record carries their resolution arm (reported, not silently dropped).

Outcomes had 0 duplicate position_ids in this snapshot (verified independently); counterfactuals had 298 duplicate position_ids (2 records each) — deduped keeping the latest timestamp (e.g. pid 2854's hold-then-scalp double-write keeps the scalp record, matching its outcome).
depth_usd_top20 coverage is 100% on pool A trades but only 75.3% on pool B (older ghosts predate the field); depth-tercile results for pool B exclude the unstamped 25%.

Ghost and outcome 'timestamp' fields are resolution/exit times, not decision times — used for both ET-day bucketing (per the stated convention) and the chronological sort feeding the L1 ATR-floor buffers; decision-to-resolution skew is <=5 min so day assignment can shift only at midnight ET boundaries.

p_l1 ATR-floor buffers warm up from the first record (static min_atr=12.0 floor until 5 samples, long-term floor until 200), exactly mirroring scheduler._kelly_bankroll_returns; pool A buffers advance over trades only, pool B over trades+ghosts, so the same trade's p_l1 can differ microscopically between pools.

regime_state 'trending_up' (the only bucket with positive raw mean_diff and >0.5 t) has n=29 — below the n>=30 bar — and t=0.81; 'volatile' n=242 t=0.46-0.62. With 44 pool-A tests, pockets like these are expected noise (multiple-comparisons count reported in output).
Day-clustered t with n_days=14 treats ET days as independent; 14 days is a thin base, and the stamped-regime buckets trending_up/trending_down have only 8/10 distinct days.
A live paper bot is appending records; counts reflect the single load at script start (2,858 outcomes, 2,801 CF records, 952 ghosts).

discrepancies: Caveat text error (non-numeric, zero effect on results): claims the p_l1 long-term ATR floor branch waits for '200' samples; both production (signal_engine._ATR_LONG_TERM_MIN_SAMPLES) and their actual code import and use 50. Sensitivity re-run with long_min=200 vs 50: pool A L1 mean and t identical to 5 decimals (-0.00950 pooled / t=-3.10), because the regime-widening branch (rolling20/long<0.60) never fires on this 14-day window.

Estimand mixing (disclosed convention, not an error, but load-bearing for interpretation): 'mean_diff' everywhere in key_numbers is the mean of ET-day means (consistent with the stated day-clustered convention and the t numerator), while the bootstrap statistic is the pooled per-record mean (-0.01100 vs day-mean -0.01126 for pool A raw). caveats: Q1 is structurally censored: every stamped decision already passed the [0.98,1.02] price-sum gate (or arrived via the Gamma fallback that bypasses it), and out-of-band ticks are skipped WITHOUT ghosting — the observed hard truncation at exactly 1.01/1.02 proves the censoring. 473,859 lifetime + 4,726 today 'stale_prices' tick-skips exist but carry no magnitude/side/fillability, and they are per-tick re-fires (one bad window re-fires every ~1s), so they cannot be converted to an opportunity count. Conclusion on the IN-BAND region (zero arb) is solid; the OUT-OF-band region is untestable with this data.

Q2's fade pool is 86% CF worst/scalp-moment rows from the bot's OWN positions — a self-selected sample of late-window moments conditioned on us having entered, not a random sample of late-window prints. Entry stamps with <90s remaining are rare (56 trade+ghost rows at >=0.85) because the time-multiplier discourages late entries. CF cheap-side cost is a proxy (1 - chosen_side_sell_price + 0.01 spread proxy), not an executable ask; CF market_price is the chosen side's SELL-side price, slightly understating the cheap side's true ask. None of this rescues the fade: the extreme side's WR matches its price within ±0.007 everywhere, so the direction of the answer is robust to the proxy.

Q3 as specified is unanswerable: depth_usd_top20 is Binance BTC top-20 depth (main.py:1146, BinanceDepthFeed.get_depth_usd), NOT Polymarket CLOB depth — no record carries CLOB depth. The table answers a weaker question (BTC-venue liquidity regime vs pricing error) and finds nothing in the thinnest quartile (t=0.40); the Q3-bucket +0.044 (t=2.97) is non-monotonic and one of 8 tested cells — multiple-comparisons noise, not a finding.

Coverage gaps reported, not silently shrunk: 29 scalped trades lack a CF record (winner unrecoverable, kept in Q1 sums but excluded from won-based stats); 24.7% of resolved decisions lack a non-zero depth stamp (older records predate the field); 0 ghosts were unresolved. Counterfactual dedupe dropped 298 duplicate position_ids (per-trade + rollup overlap / double-writes), keeping latest timestamp; outcomes had 0 duplicates at load time.

14 ET days is a thin clustering base; day-clustered t values around |4-5| on the fade are about as strong as this horizon allows, but all conclusions are paper-mode, single-market (BTC 5-min), 2026-05-28..06-10 regime only.
Prices are tick-snapped to $0.01, so s is quantized to {1.01, 1.02} in-band — sub-tick mispricing is invisible in these stamps by construction.

analysis.headline: The exit edge is real and passes the goal's bar: on 2,503 deduped CF records (1,446 scalp / 1,057 hold) over 14 ET days, actual exit policy = +$33.94 vs always-hold = -$863.27, delta = +$897.22, and the day-clustered bootstrap (1000 iters, seed 42) gives mean +$904.64, p10 +$375.95 > 0 (PASS; fee-corrected delta +$811.58 still passes at p10 +$293.92). However the honest day-clustered t is 2.08 on 14 days (1.91 fee-corrected), NOT the prior session's "~4-5 SE", and the prior LEVELS (+$1,238/+$337) reproduce exactly only WITHOUT dedupe — they double-counted +$1,204.14 of actual pnl from 298 duplicate CF records (off-by-one pid keying writes the previous position's hold-CF under the wrong id); the delta itself was robust (+$901.52 raw vs +$897.22 deduped). The edge lives entirely in ITM scalps (mp>=0.5: +$980.40, t_day 2.12) and profit scalps (+$760.25, t_day 2.60); OTM scalps are slightly value-destroying (-$83.18, t -0.50) and loss scalps' +$136.97 is insignificant (t 0.53).

caveats: Prior session's '~4-5 SE' overstates significance: the honest day-clustered t is 2.08 (n_days=14; 1.91 fee-corrected). The goal's bootstrap bar (p10 > 0) passes cleanly, but the t-statistic is borderline-2, not 4-5 — the prior SE was evidently not day-clustered.

Prior session's LEVELS (+$1,238 actual / +$337 always-hold) were computed without dedupe and double-counted +$1,204.14 of actual pnl from 298 duplicate CF records; deduped levels are +$33.94 / -$863.27. The DELTA (~+$900) was unaffected because the duplicate copies are hold-type (zero delta). The '12 days' in the prior claim was also wrong — those exact numbers reproduce on today's full 14-day pool.

DATA-QUALITY BUG (report, not fixed — polybot/** read-only this session): the counterfactual tracker keys some hold-to-resolution CF records to the PREVIOUS position's id — all 298 duplicates are (hold, scalp) pairs under one pid where the hold-copy's pnl matches the outcome of pid+1 (231/298 within $0.35) and the scalp-copy matches the pid's own outcome (244/298). This same bug explains most of the 355 outcomes (12.4% of 2,858; pnl +$1,193.38, 333 resolutions/22 scalps) that have no CF record under their own pid. Delta analysis is unaffected (missing records are hold-type, delta 0), but 'actual policy total +$33.94' on the CF pool understates total realized pnl (+$861.54 across all 2,858 outcomes).

CF records for outcomes flagged fee_restamped=0.07 (days 2026-05-28..06-04) still carry pnl at the old 0.018 fee basis — proven, not assumed: 1,567 of 1,574 nonzero per-pid gaps equal fees*(1-0.018/0.07) exactly (sum +$346.13 of the +$365.79 gap; remaining 7 mis-keyed pids sum +$19.65). The mandated 'use pnl as-is' convention therefore slightly inflates the delta on those days; the fee-corrected delta (-$85.63 exit-fee adjustment on 859 restamped scalps, entry fee cancels between arms) is +$811.58 and still passes (boot p10 +$293.92).

Delta is concentrated: top-3 days (06-04, 06-05, 06-08) contribute +$792.66 of +$897.22 (88%); best single day is 31.7% of the total; 06-07, 06-09 and 06-10 are negative. The last two days have thin n (30 and 11 scalps) — directional watch item, not significance-bearing.

The OTM-at-scalp segment (mp<0.5, n=636) has NEGATIVE delta (-$83.18, t_day -0.50): scalping out of losing positions adds no value over holding; the entire edge is in ITM/profit scalps. Loss scalps overall are +$136.97 at t_day 0.53 — not distinguishable from zero.

loss_cut split is empty: the loss_cut flag is False or absent on every scalp CF and no outcome has exit_reason='loss_cut' — the loss-cut branch never fired in this 14-day pool, so its threshold-independent contribution cannot be evaluated from data.

5 scalp-type CF records were excluded for non-binary counterfactual resolution_price; 0 records lacked pnl. The always-hold arm here is exact (binary resolution), unlike the policy-sweep replay — no replay approximation caveat applies to this comparison.

Every key_numbers entry reproduces EXACTLY, most to the cent: pool n_cf=2503 (1446 scalp/1057 hold) after dropping 298 CF duplicates (0 outcome dups; raw 2801 CF / 2858 outcomes — no mid-flight drift, counts identical); actual_total +33.94, always_hold −863.27, delta +897.22 (scalp-only arms −2622.10/−3519.31 identical); t_day 2.083~2.08 on 14 ET days (pstdev day sums, genuinely day-clustered — per-day table matches line-for-line incl. negative days 06-07/-11.21, 06-09/-107.00, 06-10/-66.18 with thin n 30/11); bootstrap (seed 42, 1000 iters, random.choices over sorted unique days, k=14) mean +904.64 p10 +375.95 p90 +1489.73 — exact match, PASS p10>0; fee-corrected: 859 restamped scalps, extra exit fee (0.07−0.018)*shares*p_exit*(1−p_exit) = 85.63, delta +811.58, t 1.912~1.91, boot +819.44/+293.92/+1389.12 PASS; splits exact (profit 525/+760.25/t2.60; loss 921/+136.97/t0.53; ITM mp>=0.5 810/+980.40/t2.12; OTM 636/−83.18/t−0.50 on 13 days; loss_cut flag n=0 and no outcome exit_reason=='loss_cut'); no-dedupe reproduction of prior-session levels exact (+1238.09/+336.57/+901.52, double-counted actual pnl +1204.14); reconciliation exact (gap +365.79 over 2503 common pids; 1567/1574 nonzero gaps == fees*(1−0.018/0.07) summing +346.13 — fee-restamp proof holds; 7 residual pids +19.65); 355 outcomes without CF (+1193.38; 333 resolution/22 scalp) and −331.84+1193.38=861.53~861.54 total realized — internally coherent; duplicate forensics exact (298 (hold,scalp) pairs, hold-copy matches outcome[pid+1] on 231, scalp-copy matches own outcome on 244 at $0.35 tol — off-by-one keying claim supported). Methodology audit: no sign errors (delta=actual−hold, positive=exit edge); dedupe correct per mandated latest-timestamp rule; day-clustering real (not per-trade SE) — the demotion of the prior '~4-5 SE' claim to t=2.08 is correct and honest; fee convention followed (pnl as-is) with the fee-corrected variant correctly touching only the scalp-arm exit fee (entry fee common to both arms, resolution fee zero at p in {0,1}); no chosen-side/P(up) confusion (market_price used only for ITM/OTM split is chosen-side from CF context); outcome.correct never used for scalp winners; always-hold arm is exact binary resolution (no replay approximation) so that caveat is sound; concentration (top-3 days 88%, best day 31.7%) and OTM-negative-delta claims reproduce and are appropriately hedged. Verdict: all headline numbers and methodology hold; only the '5 excluded' caveat sentence is wrong (true value 0).
